corpus_los: LO1: Define what a specification is and explain why it matters when working with AI.
LO2: Describe each of the four properties of a good specification: testable, bounded, observable, and actionable.
LO3: Identify whether a given specification is missing one or more of those four properties.
LO4: Rewrite a weak specification to make it stronger by applying all four properties.
LO5: Explain why AI systems need stricter specifications than human workers do.
LO6: Evaluate a specification you have written against a four-property checklist.
objective_coverage.note: q6 added in cycle 3 covers LO6 (Evaluate a specification you have written against a four-property checklist — corpus §6, §9). All 6 LOs now covered 1:1. No orphan questions. Cycle-2 blocker resolved.

beginner_fairness_and_scope.note: All stems use plain English. No undefined acronyms. Every question tests concepts explicitly taught in topic_corpus.md. Distractors represent beginner misconceptions about this topic only; no adjacent or advanced material required.

verdict: Pass — all checks clean. Positional bias max_share 0.333, length bias rate 0.0, absurd distractor rate 0.0/18, all 6 LOs covered with no orphans. Cycle-2 hard failure (LO6 uncovered) resolved: q6 added targeting LO6 (four-property checklist self-evaluation, corpus §6/§9).
